sections.heading: What is a JSON file?
body: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is an open text format that stores data as nested key-value pairs. Humans can read it, machines can process it reliably.
For the Cultural Data Space, we follow Schema.org vocabulary for events and extend it with culture-specific additions.

sections.heading: From programme to interface
body: Theatres usually publish their programmes through CMS systems. We develop adapters that automatically generate a standardised JSON interface from the CMS data model – without touching the frontend.

sections.heading: What is a connector?
body: A connector is the software bridge between a theatre system and the Cultural Data Space. It handles authentication, data format validation and the transfer protocol.
We provide reference connectors for common systems and document the protocol for in-house implementations.

sections.heading: Sending and receiving via the data space
body: The Cultural Data Space acts as broker: data senders (theatres) and data users (platforms, archives) meet here on the basis of agreed standards.
Every sender decides who may use which data – data sovereignty stays with the houses.

sections.heading: Documentation & examples
body: Schema.org Event docs, technical specification on GitHub and on the Academy Dortmund website.
Application examples: Stadttheater Augsburg, Theaterstücke FRECARO.
